topic.name: Marine Conservation Legislation
topic.description: A collection of U.S. legislative acts aimed at the conservation and management of marine resources and ecosystems.

Document 1: Amends the High Seas Driftnet Fishing Moratorium Protection Act to direct the Secretary of Commerce to list a nation in the biennial report on international compliance if the nation's fishing vessels are or have been engaged in fishing activities that target or incidentally catch sharks
makes it a prohibited act to remove any shark fin at sea
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the department in which the Coast Guard is operating to work through the International Maritime Organization to establish agreements to promote coordinated action among the United States, Russia, Canada, and other seafaring and Arctic nations
promote safe commercial maritime navigation by means of icebreaking when necessary
Document 4: Amends the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act to require fishery management plans, amendments, or regulations for overfished fisheries to specify a time period for ending overfishing and rebuilding the fishery
Directs Regional Fishery Management Councils to consider data on recreational fishermen when specifying annual catch limits in a fishery management plan
Document 5: Amends the Harmful Algal Bloom and Hypoxia Research and Control Act of 1998 to require the President to establish an Inter-Agency Task Force on Harmful Algal Blooms and Hypoxia
develop a national strategy to address marine and freshwater harmful algal bloom and hypoxia events
Document 7: Requires all new U.S. vessels with a building contract date after enactment of this Act to comply with a specified regulation requiring double hulls

Directs the Secretary to continue individual port and waterway safety assessments to determine the U.S. ports, waterways, and channels needing new or improved vessel traffic management risk mitigation measures

topic.name: Community Development and Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at enhancing community infrastructure, supporting vulnerable populations, and promoting economic development through targeted programs and grants.

Document 1: authorize the Secretary of Commerce to award a grant to a state or local government
provide access to commercial fishermen or persons in the aquaculture industry
Document 2: require states to take certain additional steps to assist children in foster care
establish individual development accounts and custodial accounts
Document 3: establish a program to make comprehensive planning grants to eligible entities
promote integrated planning and investments across policy and governmental jurisdictions
Document 4: authorize the Secretary of Labor to make formula matching grants to states
provide preparatory and supportive services, information on career and technical education
Document 5: amend the program providing formula grants to states for the special public transportation needs of older individuals
improve coordination among public and other transportation service providers
Document 6: reauthorize and make appropriations for the grant program to assist states
provide special education and related services to children with disabilities
Document 7: authorize the Secretary of Commerce to make block grants to a state or local government
assess the potential for the development or enhancement of business clusters

topic.name: Offshore Energy Policy
topic.description: The documents collectively address legislative actions and regulations related to offshore energy exploration, production, and environmental considerations in the United States.

Document 1: prohibit Secretary of the Interior from issuing a lease for the exploration, development, or production of oil or natural gas
off the coast of California, Oregon, or Washington
Document 2: repeals the excise tax on medical devices
prohibiting the issuance of new oil or natural gas production leases in the Gulf of Mexico
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to include within the schedule of proposed federal lease sales
covering wind or alternative and renewable energy as well as oil or gas exploration
Document 4: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to direct the Secretary of the Interior to make available for leasing
conduct lease sales including at least 50% of the available unleased acreage
Document 5: require the head of any federal agency to treat the issuance of any exploration plans
as a major federal action significantly affecting the quality of the human environment
Document 6: rescinds Bureau of Land Management Instruction Memoranda
direct the Secretary of the Interior to complete an economic impact assessment
Document 7: includes tar sands in the definition of 'crude oil' for purposes of the excise tax on petroleum

topic.name: Designation of Postal Facilities
topic.description: The documents focus on the designation and redesignation of various postal facilities and federal buildings in the United States, often honoring notable individuals.

Document 1: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 103 Center Street West in Eatonville, Washington, as the 'National Park Ranger Margaret Anderson Post Office.'
Document 2: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 6531 Van Nuys Boulevard in Van Nuys, California, as the 'Marilyn Monroe Post Office.'
Document 3: Redesignates the federal building and courthouse located at 200 East Wall Street in Midland, Texas, known as the George Mahon Federal Building, as the 'George H.W. Bush and George W. Bush United States Courthouse and George Mahon Federal Building.'
Document 4: Designates the federal building and U.S. courthouse located at 224 South Boulder Avenue in Tulsa, Oklahoma, as the 'H. Dale Cook Federal Building and United States Courthouse.'
Document 6: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 4110 Almeda Road in Houston, Texas, as the 'George Thomas 'Mickey' Leland Post Office Building.'
Document 7: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 744 West Oglethorpe Highway in Hinesville, Georgia, as the 'John Sidney 'Sid' Flowers Post Office Building.'

topic.name: Judicial and Counterterrorism Reforms
topic.description: This cluster discusses legislative amendments related to judicial procedures, transparency, and counterterrorism authorities in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the USA PATRIOT Improvement and Reauthorization Act of 2005 to extend through February 29, 2012, a provision granting roving electronic surveillance authority.
revising the definition of an 'agent of a foreign power' to include any non-U.S. person who engages in international terrorism.
Document 2: Amends the federal judicial code to prohibit a court from entering an order restricting the disclosure of information obtained through discovery.
restricting access to court records, unless in connection with such order the court has first made independent findings of fact.
Document 3: Amends the USA PATRIOT Improvement and Reauthorization Act of 2005 and the Intelligence Reform and Terrorism Prevention Act of 2004 to extend through December 31, 2013, provisions granting roving surveillance authority.
authorizing the Director of the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) to apply for a court order requiring production of tangible things.
Document 4: Amends the federal judicial code to declare that, with respect to diversity of citizenship, the U.S. district courts shall not have original jurisdiction of any civil action between citizens of a state and citizens or subjects of a foreign state.
modifies the citizenship rules to treat corporations as citizens of any foreign state.
Document 5: Authorizes the presiding judge of a U.S. appellate court or U.S. district court to permit the photographing, electronic recording, broadcasting, or televising to the public of court proceedings.
Prohibits the presiding judge from permitting the photographing, electronic recording, broadcasting, or televising of any juror in a trial proceeding.
Document 6: Amends the federal judicial code to establish the Office of Inspector General for the Judicial Branch.
Requires the office to conduct investigations of alleged misconduct in the judicial branch.
Document 7: Provides that an action may be brought only in federal court for actual, not punitive, damages against the Army Corps of Engineers.
Establishes the Federal Engineering Accountability Commission to determine the Corps' failure or negligence after such a disaster.

topic.name: Medicare and Medicaid Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative amendments related to Medicare and Medicaid programs, addressing various aspects of healthcare coverage and provider regulations.

Document 1: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act (SSA) with respect to the formula for calculating payments for physicians' services.
Extends the current quality reporting system.
Document 2: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act (SSAct) to require a state Medicaid plan to provide 12-month continuous enrollment for an eligible individual.
Document 3: Amends part D (Voluntary Prescription Drug Benefit Program) of title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services to negotiate with pharmaceutical manufacturers to reduce the purchase cost of covered Medicare part D drugs.
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to make citizens of Puerto Rico eligible for the federal earned income tax credit and allow them to claim the refundable portion of the child tax credit.
Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act (SSAct) to terminate the limitations on general Medicaid funding.
Document 5: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to establish limits on the removal of Medicare Advantage (MA) providers by MA organizations.
Establishes network adequacy requirements.
Document 6: Amends SSA title II (Old Age, Survivors and Disability Insurance) (OASDI) to continue entitlement to prescription drugs used in immunosuppressive therapy furnished to an individual who receives a kidney transplant.
Applies deductible and coinsurance requirements to the provision of such drugs.
Document 7: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to cover hearing aids and auditory rehabilitation services under the Medicare program.

topic.name: Social Security and Disability Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts related to Social Security, disability insurance, and employment leave for individuals affected by health-related issues or family violence.

Document 1: amends title II of the Social Security Act
eliminate the waiting period for disability insurance benefits for terminally ill individuals
Document 2: Amends the Family and Medical Leave Act
entitle an eligible employee to leave during court proceedings for violent crimes
Document 3: Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act
prohibit the accrual of deferred compensation to a disqualified individual
Document 4: reestablish the Social Security Administration's disability insurance demonstration project authority
Document 5: eliminate the two-year waiting period following a divorce
for a divorced spouse to become entitled to a spouse's OASDI benefits
Document 6: Amends title XVI of the Social Security Act
increase resource limits for aged, blind, or disabled individuals
Document 7: Amends the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act
address potential shortages in the Multiemployer Health Benefit Plan

topic.name: Higher Education Loan Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative amendments and acts related to student loans and financial assistance in higher education.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to appoint a Private Education Loan Ombudsman to provide timely assistance to borrowers of private education loans.
compile and analyze data on borrower complaints regarding private education loans.
Document 2: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to make the following entities, programs, and courses eligible for funding under title IV.
Lists the items to be included in a state's alternative accreditation agreement with the Secretary.
Document 3: This bill amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to permit student loan borrowers to refinance their loans at interest rates offered on loans to banks by the Federal Reserve System.
Document 4: This bill amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to modify provisions related to the Federal Pell Grant program.
increases the maximum Federal Pell Grant award for academic year 2017-2018 and adjusts it in subsequent award years.
Document 5: This bill amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to adjust the maximum annual and aggregate amount that a student may borrow under the Federal Perkins Loan program to account for inflation.
Document 6: Amends the Higher Education Extension Act of 2005 to extend the programs under the Higher Education Act of 1965 through September 30, 2007.
Document 7: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to allow members of the Armed Forces and National Guard to defer payments of principal on their Federal Family Education Loans.

topic.name: Aviation and Weather Safety Regulations
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislation related to aviation safety, weather monitoring, and navigation standards to enhance public safety in air travel and maritime operations.

Document 1: Metropolitan Weather Hazards Protection Act of 2015 directs the Department of Commerce to maintain and operate Doppler weather radar sites.
Commerce shall report to Congress on the Doppler weather radar sites that need to be constructed.
Document 2: Federal Aviation Administration Extension Act of 2008 extends funding for various airport development projects.
Amends federal transportation laws to extend funding through September 30, 2008, for various airport development projects.
Document 3: Airline Safety and Federal Aviation Administration Extension Act of 2010 extends project grant authority for the airport improvement program.
Extends air carrier liability limits for injuries to passengers resulting from acts of terrorism.
Document 4: Vessel Navigation and Safety Improvement Act directs the Coast Guard's Commandant to issue regulations requiring pilots on commercial vessels to use portable electronic devices.
Document 5: Pilot's Bill of Rights requires NTSB proceedings for review of FAA decisions regarding airman certificates.
Directs the Administrator to improve the system of providing airmen with pertinent and timely information before a flight.
Document 6: Amends the Vision 100-Century of Aviation Reauthorization Act to extend the effective period of final orders regarding essential air service subsidies.
Document 7: Silent Skies Act of 2015 directs the FAA to develop aircraft technology to comply with noise standards.
DOT must issue regulations to ensure turbojets comply with stage 4 noise levels by specified dates.

topic.name: Victim Support and Safety Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at supporting victims of violence and enhancing safety measures in various communities, particularly focusing on domestic violence, campus safety, and community policing.

Document 1: Sets forth entitlement standards and implementation guidelines for employee use of emergency leave to address domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, or stalking.
Prohibits denial or restriction of insurance coverage based on the status of the applicant or insured as a victim of domestic or sexual violence.
Document 2: Directs the Attorney General to establish a national Silver Alert communications network to assist search efforts for missing seniors.
Document 4: Requires institutions of higher education to include in their annual security report a statement of current policies for reporting crimes or other emergencies.
Requires such procedures to include the provision, in writing, to students or employees that report that they have been the victim of such an offense certain information regarding their rights.

topic.name: Renewable Energy Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on U.S. legislation aimed at promoting renewable energy sources and improving energy efficiency.

Document 1: Sets forth a schedule for motor vehicle fuel to contain specified volumes of renewable fuel by calendar year 2022.
Establishes a grants program for renewable fuel production.
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of Defense to procure domestically produced liquid fuel suitable for aviation that is derived from biomass.
Requires the Secretary to report to Congress on performance under each such contract.
Document 5: Revises corporate average fuel economy standards for automobiles and light trucks to improve fuel economy.
Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow an energy efficient motor vehicles manufacturing tax credit.

topic.name: Employment and Labor Rights
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at protecting the rights of employees in various contexts, including volunteer services, safety regulations, and voting participation.

Document 1: Prohibits any employee from being terminated, demoted, or discriminated against in the terms or conditions of employment because the employee is absent or late as a result of serving as a volunteer firefighter or providing volunteer emergency medical services.
Gives such an employee a private cause of action for discrimination which violates this Act.
Document 2: Requires the Secretary of Labor to determine why an accident occurred and whether there were violations of law, mandatory health and safety standards, or other requirements.
Authorizes appropriations.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to require the Secretary of the Treasury to notify the Secretary of Homeland Security if an employee's tax identification number indicates that such employee is not authorized to be employed in the United States.
Grants an affected employee the right to contest the accuracy of the Secretary's notice.
Document 4: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to provide that nothing in specified prohibitions against unfair labor practices by employers shall be construed as requiring an employer to employ any person who seeks or has sought employment.
Document 5: Entitles executive agency employees to leave, without loss of or reduction in pay, to provide election administration assistance.
Requires reports to the Commission and to Congress on activities conducted with funds provided under this Act.
Document 6: Amends the National Voter Registration Act of 1993 to require designating direct each Secretary of a military department to designate an office on each Armed Forces installation to ensure that certain individuals are provided the opportunity to register to vote.
Requires informing Armed Forces members and their dependents of the assistance available under this Act.
Document 7: Requires the Secretary of Labor to determine why an accident occurred and whether there were violations of law, mandatory health and safety standards, or other requirements.
Allows the closest relative of a miner who is entrapped or otherwise prevented by an accident to designate a representative for the miner to participate in a mine inspection.

topic.name: Veterans Health Care Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving and expanding health care services and support for veterans.

Document 1: Prohibits the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) from altering the health care available to a veteran who is enrolled in the VA health care system.
the amount of time that veteran has to wait for an appointment for such care based solely on the length of time since he or she last received health care from the VA.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to carry out a pilot program to provide veterans' readjustment counseling and related mental health services.
Requires the Secretary to establish two mobile Vet Centers in each of five specified Veterans Integrated Service Network areas.
Document 3: Authorizes the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to place such veteran in a medical foster home that meets VA standards.
Amends the Veterans Access, Choice, and Accountability Act of 2014 to reduce the aggregate amount of awards and bonuses that may be paid by the VA.
Document 4: Modifies provisions relating to health care services for veterans to require the Secretary of the Veterans Administration (VA) to furnish health care services to veterans who do not qualify under existing VA programs.
Directs the VA Secretary to use the VA capitation-based resource allocation model in entering into contracts for furnishing health care services.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to establish the VetStar Award Program, to annually recognize businesses for their contributions to veterans' employment.
Document 6: Makes permanent the authority of the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to furnish hospital care, medical services, and nursing home care for any illness to a veteran who participated in a test conducted by the Department of Defense.
Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to contract with the Institute of Medicine of the National Academies to conduct an expanded study on the health impact of Project SHAD.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to establish a national cemetery in the Sarpy County, Nebraska, region to serve the needs of veterans and their families.

topic.name: Energy Infrastructure Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative measures related to the construction and regulation of energy infrastructure projects in the United States.

Document 1: Authorizes TransCanada Keystone Pipeline, L.P. to construct, connect, operate, and maintain the pipeline and cross-border facilities specified in an application.
Grants original and exclusive jurisdiction... to the U.S. Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit over any civil action for the review of a federal agency action regarding the pipeline.
Document 2: Directs the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC)... to reinstate the license and extend to September 25, 2013, the time period during which the licensee is required to commence the construction of project works.
Document 3: Requires the Nuclear Regulatory Commission to retain amounts collected for safety-related fines and to distribute them to counties for maintaining radiological emergency preparedness plans.
Document 4: Amends the Energy Reorganization Act of 1974 to permit any Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) member... to continue to serve until the earlier of... the date on which the member's successor has been appointed.
Document 5: Authorizes TransCanada Keystone Pipeline, L.P. to construct, connect, operate, and maintain the pipeline and cross-border facilities specified in an application.

Grants the United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit... original and exclusive jurisdiction over any civil action for the review of any order or action of a federal agency regarding the pipeline.
Document 6: Exempts any project determined by the Secretary of the Interior to be an offshore meteorological site testing and monitoring project from environmental impact statement requirements.
Defines an 'offshore meteorological site testing and monitoring project' as a project that is administered by the Department of the Interior.
Document 7: Requires the President to designate not less than three closed military installations... appropriate for siting a refinery for gasoline or other fuel.
Gives the U.S. Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia exclusive jurisdiction over civil actions relating to federal refinery authorizations.

topic.name: Drug Policy Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative acts related to drug policy, including taxation, penalties, and regulatory measures concerning controlled substances.

Document 1: impose an excise tax on the sale of marijuana
civil penalties for failure to comply
Document 2: impose enhanced criminal penalties for serious violent felonies committed during drug trafficking
additional restrictions on the release of persons charged with firearms offenses
Document 3: provide access to criminal history information for licensing of financial service providers
criminal history background checks required under state law
Document 4: impose a fine and/or prison term for intentional failure to file financial information
Document 5: deny the transfer of firearms to individuals engaged in terrorist activities
prohibits sale or distribution of firearms to individuals determined to be engaged in terrorism
Document 6: requires the Attorney General's prior written approval for federal prosecution of drug offenses
modifies CSA and CSIEA to delete specified mandatory minimum terms
Document 7: make it unlawful to knowingly sponsor or exhibit an animal in dog fighting
provides for imprisonment for violations

topic.name: Consumer Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at protecting consumers' rights and personal information in different contexts.

Document 7: Amends the Fair Credit Reporting Act to require nationwide consumer reporting agencies to provide a free annual credit score disclosure upon consumer request.

topic.name: Tax Incentives for Public Benefit
topic.description: This cluster discusses various tax-related legislative acts aimed at promoting public benefits such as renewable energy, education, and support for small businesses.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to tax-exempt new clean renewable energy bonds
allow such unlimited bonds to be issued until January 1, 2014
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a tax credit for 30% of amounts paid for WaterSense program property
Terminates such credit after 2010
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow rehabilitation expenditures for a qualified public educational facility
qualify for the rehabilitation tax credit
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to the tax credit for the health insurance expenses of small employers
expand eligibility for the credit to employers having up to 50 full-time equivalent employees
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through 2010 the tax credit for differential wage payments
made by a small business employer on behalf of employees who are active duty members of the uniformed services
Document 6: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow all taxpayers a tax deduction for expenses relating to the home schooling of their children
at the elementary or secondary school level
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exempt the holdings of a private foundation in any business enterprise
from the excise taxes on excess business holdings

topic.name: Environmental Restoration Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative acts aimed at environmental restoration and management in specific regions of the United States.

Document 1: Lake Tahoe Restoration Act to conduct forest management activities in the Lake Tahoe Basin
support the attainment of the environmental threshold carrying capacities.
Document 3: Chesapeake Bay Accountability and Recovery Act requires a financial report for restoration activities
adaptive management plan for restoration activities in the Chesapeake Bay watershed.
Document 4: Flood Protection Public Safety Act authorizes flood risk management projects
project must be technically sound, environmentally acceptable, and economically justified.
Document 5: Upper Mississippi Conservation and River Protection Act directs study of Asian carp management
assess potential environmental and economic impacts.
Document 6: Chesapeake Bay Restoration Act requires permits for stormwater discharges
ensures projects comply with stormwater management standards.
Document 7: American Levee Certification Act directs levee system evaluation and certification
limits non-federal share of costs for levee evaluations.

topic.name: Iran Sanctions Legislation
topic.description: The documents collectively address various U.S. legislative measures aimed at imposing sanctions on Iran related to its ballistic missile program, petroleum resources, and proliferation activities.

Document 1: amends the Iran-Iraq Arms Non-Proliferation Act of 1992 to cover ballistic missile or related technology
require sanctions on Iran and related entities for actions to acquire or develop ballistic missiles
Document 2: authorize the Secretary of Health and Human Services to exclude from participation in any federal health care program entities affiliated with a sanctioned entity
Document 5: maintain specified U.S. sanctions with respect to Iran until the President certifies that Iran has verifiably dismantled its weapons of mass destruction programs
includes transshipment among sanctionable activities
Document 6: direct the President to impose two or more sanctions if a person has made an investment of $20 million or more that contributed to Iran's ability to develop its petroleum resources
Document 7: expresses the sense of Congress that the United States should use diplomatic and economic measures to resolve the Iranian nuclear problem
prohibits direct or indirect importation of Iranian articles into the United States

topic.name: Advancements in Technology and Innovation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at enhancing research, development, and infrastructure for various technological innovations across multiple sectors.

Document 1: Enhancing Safety in Medicine Utilizing Leading Advanced Simulation Technologies
requires the Director of the Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality to conduct and support research
Document 2: directs the Secretary of Commerce to establish a Nanomanufacturing Investment Partnership
Requires establishment of nanotechnology research grant programs
Document 3: direct the Secretary of Commerce to award grants for the development of feasibility studies
Allows the Secretary to guarantee up to 80% of the loan amount for loans exceeding $10 million
Document 5: establish a research and development program to provide data and scientific analyses
analyze and assess current and future critical minerals and materials supply chains
Document 6: conduct prize competitions to accelerate the development and commercialization of technology
improves spectrum efficiency and is capable of cost-effective deployment
Document 7: direct the Secretary of Commerce to award grants for the development of feasibility studies
review and recommend best practices for the development of U.S. science parks

topic.name: U.S. International Assistance Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on U.S. international aid and development efforts across various regions and issues.

Document 1: prohibit the United States Agency for International Development (USAID) from procuring manufactured articles for disaster or emergency assistance in a foreign country
manufactured in such foreign country or, under certain circumstances, in the United States or third countries.
Document 2: authorize the President to provide assistance, including through international organizations
support treatment of obstetric fistula.
Document 4: U.S. policy, in partnership with the government of Haiti
support of Haiti's reconstruction.
Document 6: encourage the efforts of countries in sub-Saharan Africa to implement national power strategies
provide access to reliable, affordable, and sustainable power.
Document 7: assist countries in sub-Saharan Africa develop an appropriate mix of power solutions
alleviate poverty and drive economic growth.

topic.name: Agricultural Policy Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at reforming and managing agricultural programs and funding in the United States.

Document 1: Establishes the National Institutes for Food and Agriculture
Extends and revises specified agricultural programs
Document 2: Amends the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008
Replaces the average crop revenue election program with the ARRM program
Document 3: Amends the Consolidated Farm and Rural Development Act to expand eligibility for loans
Deems specified entities eligible for farm ownership loans
Document 4: Suspends the prohibition on the authority of the Secretary of Agriculture to guarantee an agricultural operating loan
Document 5: Caps maximum annual direct and counter-cyclical payments to an individual
Revises limitation provisions for marketing loan gains
Document 6: Amends the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008 with respect to producers
Revises the definition of 'disaster county'
Document 7: Authorizes appropriations for the McGovern-Dole International Food for Education Program
Specifies fund amounts to be used for the Program

topic.name: Federal Recognition of Tribes
topic.description: This cluster discusses various acts that extend federal recognition and associated benefits to specific Native American tribes in the United States.

Document 1: Extends federal recognition to the Little Shell Tribe of Chippewa Indians of Montana.
Makes the Tribe and each member eligible for all services and benefits provided by the United States to Indians.
Document 2: Extends all federal laws and regulations to the Duwamish Tribe.
Declares the Tribe and its members eligible for all services and benefits provided by the federal government.
Document 3: Extends federal recognition and associated services and benefits to the Mowa Band of Choctaw Indians of Alabama.
Restores federal rights and privileges abrogated by earlier statutes.
Document 4: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to make annual disbursements to the Colorado River Indian Tribes.
Amends the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act to authorize the Alaska Native Corporation to issue additional settlement common stock.
Document 5: Amends the Act commonly known as the Indian Reorganization Act to apply the Act to all federally recognized Indian tribes.
Overrules the Supreme Court's decision in Carcieri v. Salazar.
Document 6: Amends the Compact of Free Association Amendments Act of 2003 with respect to the provision of emergency and disaster assistance.
Provides for the continuation of legal services to the citizens of Micronesia, Palau, and the Marshall Islands.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to take into trust for the benefit of the Burns Paiute Tribe.
Requires such land to be considered to be part of the Burns Paiute Reservation.

topic.name: U.S. Foreign Policy and Security
topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of U.S. foreign policy, focusing on human rights, counterterrorism, and international cooperation in security matters.

Document 1: the United States aspires to a mutually beneficial relationship with Venezuela based on respect for human rights and the rule of law
the government's failure to guarantee public security has led Venezuela to become one of the most violent countries in the world
Document 2: U.S. policy to promote the signing, ratification, and implementation by all countries in the Western Hemisphere of specified counter terrorism conventions
States U.S. policy with respect to the prevention of the proliferation of weapons-related nuclear, chemical, and biological materials
Document 3: requires the President to transmit to Congress a report, an evaluation, and a strategy on U.S. efforts to combat terrorists' and terrorist organizations' use of social media
an assessment of any impact the efforts of the federal government to combat terrorists' and terrorist organizations' use of social media may have upon the civil rights and liberties of U.S. persons
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of State to deny issuance of a visa and the Secretary of Homeland Security to deny U.S. admission to any government official of Iran who is credibly alleged to have ordered, acquiesced to, or participated in human rights abuses

States that the establishment and carrying out of operations by U.S. nonprofit organizations in Iran for the direct provision of specified humanitarian and people-to-people assistance may not be prohibited or restricted
Document 5: foreign governments, including the governments of the Russian Federation and China, use disinformation and other propaganda tools to undermine the national security objectives of the United States
the U.S. government should develop a comprehensive strategy to counter foreign disinformation and propaganda
Document 6: Approves the United States-India Agreement for Cooperation on Peaceful Uses of Nuclear Energy
States that licenses may be issued by the Nuclear Regulatory Commission for transfers pursuant to the Agreement only after the President certifies to Congress
Document 7: Amends the Arms Export Control Act to include the Republic of Korea among those countries given preferential consideration with respect to certain arms export presidential certification
military training reporting requirements

topic.name: Federal Regulatory Reforms
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative reforms aimed at improving federal regulatory processes and oversight.

Document 1: Amends title II of the Social Security Act to provide procedures for totalization agreements with other countries.
Document 2: Amends the Administrative Procedure Act to revise requirements for federal agency rulemaking.
Document 3: Grants a temporary stay of any litigation, liens, or other collection actions to recover debts owed by the government of Puerto Rico.
Document 4: Requires federal agencies to review their covered rules and modify, consolidate, or terminate such rules on a periodic basis.
Document 5: Amends title XVIII of the Social Security Act to revise the process by which Medicare administrative contractors issue local coverage determinations.
Document 6: Requires the head of each federal agency to submit a monthly report for each rule expected to propose or finalize.
Document 7: Permits foreign aircraft to provide and sustain air commerce in American Samoa.

topic.name: Tax Policy and Reform
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at reforming tax regulations and protecting taxpayer rights in the United States.

Document 1: 1099K Overreach Prevention Act - Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exempt taxpayers...
Document 2: Taxpayer Protection Act of 2014 - Amends the Internal Revenue Code to require the annual report...
Document 3: Establishes in the Treasury the National Military Family Relief Fund to make grants to Armed Forces members...
Document 4: Marketplace Equity Act of 2011 - Authorizes states to require all sellers making remote sales to collect and remit sales and use taxes...
Document 5: FairTax Act of 2015 - This bill is a tax reform proposal that imposes a national sales tax...
Document 6: This bill directs the Internal Revenue Service to implement security measures...
Document 7: Free File Program Act of 2011 - Authorizes and directs the Secretary of the Treasury to continue to operate...

topic.name: Economic Recovery Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on regulating financial practices and funding related to economic recovery and community development in the wake of financial crises.

Document 1: Imposes an additional income tax on bonuses paid to employees or former employees of covered Troubled Asset Relief Program (TARP) recipients.
Requires tax revenues generated by this Act to be paid to the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) to fund community development programs.
Document 2: Defines as 'critical to the national economy' an activity or function that promotes U.S. economic growth by providing access to information that stimulates innovation and discovery.
Authorizes Commerce to continue to maintain a permanent repository of nonclassified scientific, technical, and engineering information.
Document 3: Prohibits the Federal National Mortgage Association (Fannie Mae) and the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation (Freddie Mac) from making any contribution or transfer to, or allocating or setting aside any amounts for, the Housing Trust Fund.
Requires any amounts paid or repaid to the Secretary of the Treasury by a GSE to be covered into the General Fund and for the same purpose only.
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to impose an excise tax on excessive bonuses paid by federal emergency economic assistance recipients to their employees.
Defines 'excessive bonus' as any retention bonus or other bonus that exceeds $50,000 paid by a federal emergency economic assistance recipient after December 31, 2008.

topic.name: Health and Welfare Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at improving health insurance coverage, child support enforcement, and access to nutritious meals for children.

Document 1: prohibit a group health plan or health insurance issuer offering group health coverage from denying benefits for the treatment of an injury
resulted from participation in a legal mode of transportation or recreational activity.
Document 2: mandatory revocation of passports of individuals who are more than $5,000 in arrears in child support payments.
Document 3: establish under a new title XXII a program to provide comprehensive health insurance coverage to all Americans
not covered under certain federal health insurance programs.
Document 4: increase reimbursement rates for free, reduced price, and paid meals and snacks
improve the ability of the CACFP to reach low-income families.
Document 5: allow amounts in a flexible spending arrangement (FSA) that are not spent for medical care to be distributed to the FSA participant as taxable income
includes such FSAs in the definition of tax-exempt cafeteria plans.
Document 6: allow qualified employers under such Act to offer their employees enrollment in a health benefits plan
when fewer than two qualified health plans are offered through the Small Business Health Options Program.
Document 7: permit a health plan to decline coverage of specific items and services that are contrary to the religious beliefs of the sponsor
creates a private cause of action for the protection of individual rights created under this Act.

topic.name: Public Health Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving various aspects of public health through federal initiatives and funding.

Document 1: Designates the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) as the primary federal agency for emergency medical services and trauma care.
Establishes the Office of Emergency Medical Services and Trauma within HHS.
Document 2: Prohibits the Department of Health and Human Service (HHS) from providing federal family planning assistance to an entity unless the entity certifies that...
Excludes an abortion where: (1) the pregnancy is the result of rape or incest; or (2) a physician certifies that the woman suffered from a physical disorder...
Document 3: Allows the Secretary of Health and Human Services to make grants to states and Indian tribes for colorectal cancer programs.
Providing colorectal cancer screening to individuals who are over 50 years of age or at high risk for such cancer.
Document 4: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to establish and implement programs for the identification, prevention, and treatment of problem and pathological gambling.
Requires the Secretary to carry out a national campaign to increase knowledge and raise awareness of problem gambling.
Document 5: Amends title V (Maternal and Child Health Services) of the Social Security Act to expand provisions for abstinence education.
Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish a pilot program to make grants to public and nonprofit entities.
Document 6: Requires the Director of the National Institute of Mental Health to use funds made available by this Act exclusively for the purpose of conducting and supporting research on the determinants of self-directed and other violence.
Studies designed to reduce the risk of self-harm, suicide, and interpersonal violence.
Document 7: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to allow the Secretary of Health and Human Services to deny a new drug application if the application fails to include required information on clinical investigations.
Requires the Secretary to develop guidance for the staff of the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to ensure that new drug applications are adequately reviewed.

topic.name: Government Oversight and Policy
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative measures aimed at improving government oversight, policy implementation, and the management of federal programs.

Document 1: Instructs the Secretary of Energy to study and report to Congress on the use of thorium-fueled nuclear reactors for national energy needs.
Requires such report to include a response to the International Atomic Energy Agency study.
Document 2: Amends the Help America Vote Act of 2002 to terminate the Election Assistance Commission.
Transfers specified election administration functions of the EAC to the Federal Election Commission.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to develop and implement a national strategy for identifying and evaluating health needs of NHOPI populations.
Conducts a preliminary health survey to identify major regions where NHOPI people reside.
Document 4: Directs the Chief of the National Guard Bureau to serve as an advocate and liaison for the National Guard.
Requires the Chief to consult with state governors before changes are made in National Guard force structure.
Document 5: Requires that reports to Congress by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs be submitted in electronic format.
Document 6: Amends the National Defense Authorization Act to revise the authority of the Department of Defense to establish exchange programs.
Requires coordination of SPP regulations with combatant commanders.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Defense to establish a Quality Assurance Surveillance Plan for security contractors.
Requires the Comptroller General to assess DOD compliance with the plan.

topic.name: U.S. National Security Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the detention, treatment, and management of individuals associated with terrorism and national security concerns in the United States.

Document 1: high-value enemy combatant captured or otherwise taken into long-term custody or detention by the United States
detained at the Guantanamo Bay Detention Facility
Document 5: prohibit anyone from performing lobbying activities on behalf of a client which is a country whose government has repeatedly provided support for acts of international terrorism
Document 6: authorize the Secretary of the Treasury to issue United States War Bonds to aid in the funding of the operations in Iraq and Afghanistan
military operations abroad identified by the Secretary of Defense as being part of the international conflicts against terrorism
Document 7: prohibits the use of funds to transfer, release, or assist in the transfer or release to or within the United States of Khalid Sheikh Mohammed
No funds may be used to close or abandon Guantanamo

topic.name: Transportation Safety Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and regulations aimed at improving safety standards and practices in various modes of transportation, including pipelines and rail systems.

Document 1: Prescribes or revises requirements for safety and environmental protection in pipeline transportation.
Subjects to a civil penalty of $250,000 any person that the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) has found to have committed a major consequence violation.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) to revise federal regulations regarding DOT-111 tank cars used to move flammable liquids.
Require additional safety upgrades to tank cars built after October 2011.
Document 3: Requires states, at a minimum, to cooperate with affected nonmetropolitan local officials responsible for transportation through regional transportation planning organizations.
Document 5: Revises requirements for the apportionment of state planning funds to metropolitan planning organizations (MPOs).
Specifies criteria the Secretary shall consider in making such designation.
Document 7: Reauthorizes through FY2019 the gas and hazardous liquid pipeline and related programs of the Department of Transportation (DOT).
DOT shall periodically report to Congress on specific actions taken to implement such mandates.

topic.name: Water Resource Management in California
topic.description: The documents focus on federal legislation and initiatives related to the management, reclamation, and restoration of water resources in California.

Document 1: Directs the Department of the Interior to enter into negotiations for the transfer of ownership, control, and operation of the New Melones Unit, Central Valley Project to interested local water and power providers within California.
Defines 'interested local water and power providers' to include various irrigation districts.
Document 2: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior to participate in the design, planning, and construction of projects to treat, reclaim, and reuse impaired surface water from Lake Hodges in San Diego County, California.
Limits the federal share to 25% of the total project costs.
Document 3: This bill amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Department of the Interior to participate in the design, planning, and construction of recycled water system facilities in California.
The federal share of each project is limited to 25%.
Document 4: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to conduct a feasibility study on raising the height of the Arthur V. Watkins Dam for the development of additional storage to meet water supply needs.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to implement the terms and conditions of the Stipulation of Settlement in cooperation with the state of California, including measures to design and construct channel and structural improvements.
Provides for the reintroduction of Chinook salmon into the San Joaquin River.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to prepare feasibility studies for the design, planning, and construction of all projects included in the Friant Water Users Authority document.
Carry out any projects and programs determined to be feasible.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to implement the Stipulation of Settlement in cooperation with the state of California, including measures to acquire water rights from willing sellers.
Authorizes the Secretary to acquire property needed to implement the Settlement.

topic.name: Environmental Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at regulating environmental standards, particularly concerning air and water quality, and reducing exposure to harmful substances like lead and greenhouse gases.

Document 1: Amends the Safe Drinking Water Act to direct the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to review and revise the national primary drinking water regulation for lead.
Requires community water systems to replace a percentage of non-lead free service lines.
Document 2: Expresses the sense of Congress that the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) should take into account the effect of numeric nutrient criteria for nitrogen or phosphorus.
Prohibits the Administrator from proposing, promulgating, or enforcing numeric nutrient criteria for any stream, lake, spring, canal, estuary, or marine water of Florida.
Document 3: Amends the Toxic Substances Control Act (TSCA) to exclude from the definition of 'abatement' any renovation, remodeling, or other activity that incidentally results in a reduction or elimination of lead-based paint hazards.
Requires the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to study the extent to which persons engaged in such activities are exposed to lead.
Document 4: Amends the Clean Air Act to establish national pollutant tonnage limitations for sulfur dioxide, nitrogen oxides, mercury, and carbon dioxide.
Directs the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to establish a nitrogen oxide allowance trading program.
Document 5: Requires the President to reduce greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions relative to 2005 levels by specific percentages by 2020, 2025, 2030, and 2050.
Defines a 'first seller' as an entity in the business of producing or importing fossil carbon.
Document 6: Amends the Clean Air Act to modify provisions regarding geographical areas that do not attain the national ambient air quality standards (NAAQS) for ozone and carbon monoxide.
Directs the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to require each state to revise the boundaries of all nonattainment areas.
Document 7: Prohibits the head of a federal agency from promulgating any regulation relating to power sector carbon pollution standards unless explicitly authorized by Congress.
Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to require the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to base permit determinations only on compliance with regulations.

topic.name: LGBTQ+ Rights Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative measures aimed at protecting and promoting the rights of individuals based on their sexual orientation and gender identity.

Document 1: prohibit discrimination on the basis of sexual orientation or gender identity in housing sales and rentals
prohibit the intimidation, interference, or injury of individuals because of their sexual orientation or gender identity.
Document 2: Prohibits employment discrimination on the basis of actual or perceived sexual orientation or gender identity
Prohibits related retaliation.
Document 5: Amends the Defense of Marriage Act to repeal a provision that prohibited a state from being required to recognize same sex marriage
removes the definition of 'spouse' for purposes of federal law.
Document 6: Prohibits public school students from being excluded from participating based on sexual orientation or gender identity
Considers harassment to be a form of discrimination.
Document 7: prohibit the exclusion of citizens from service as a juror on account of sexual orientation or gender identity
expanding the current prohibition on exclusion on account of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, or economic status.

topic.name: U.S. Immigration Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative acts related to the U.S. immigration system, focusing on adjustments to immigration laws and policies for different categories of immigrants and nonimmigrants.

Document 1: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to eliminate the per country numerical limitation for employment-based immigrants
increase the per country numerical limitation for family based immigrants.
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of Homeland Security to admit into the United States as a visitor for pleasure a qualifying Canadian citizen over 55 years old
Requires such visitor also to be not inadmissible or described in any ground of deportability.
Document 3: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to require distribution of state criminal alien assistance program funds
no later than 120 days after the last day of the annual program application period.
Document 4: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to include as a nonimmigrant alien an alien who is the parent of a child who is a U.S. citizen
Defines 'parent' to include a legal guardian or other person standing in loco parentis.
Document 5: Amends the William Wilberforce Trafficking Victims Protection Reauthorization Act of 2008
Directs the Secretary of State to negotiate agreements regarding the repatriation of children.
Document 6: Extends U.S. immigration laws to the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands
Sets forth provisions respecting numerical limitations for nonimmigrant workers.
Document 7: This bill amends the Immigration and Nationality Act (INA) to make an alien, other than a lawful permanent resident, who is identified in the terrorist screening database inadmissible or deportable on terrorist grounds
DHS shall revise specified regulations implementing the United Nations Convention Against Torture.

topic.name: Military Benefits and Support
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to the benefits and support provided to members of the Armed Forces and their families.

Document 1: Redesignates the Department of the Navy as the Department of the Navy and Marine Corps.
Makes similar redesignations with respect to Department Assistant Secretaries.
Document 2: Deems as active-duty service the service of a person entitled to retired pay for nonregular service.
Provides benefits through the Department of Veterans Affairs.
Document 3: Expresses the sense of Congress that members of the Armed Forces killed or wounded in the attack at Fort Hood should receive the same treatment and benefits as those affected by September 11.
Treats members and DOD civilian employees killed or wounded in the shootings at Fort Hood as members killed or wounded in a combat zone.
Document 4: Requires the Secretary of Defense to provide a one-on-one mental health assessment for each member of the Armed Forces deployed.
Ensures assessments are conducted using an Internet-based questionnaire if necessary.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of Defense to provide postal benefit vouchers for members of the Armed Forces on active duty.
Entitles voucher users to make free mailings to such individuals.
Document 6: Authorizes the burial in a national cemetery of a parent of a deceased veteran.
Applies when the parent does not have a spouse, surviving spouse, or child eligible for interment.
Document 7: Amends federal criminal code to allow tobacco products to be mailed to a member of the Armed Forces serving in a combat zone.

topic.name: Suspension of Tariff Duties
topic.description: The documents discuss the temporary suspension of tariff duties on various chemical substances and formulations in the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on 2-butyne-1,4-diol
polymer with (chloromethyl)oxirane
Document 2: extend the temporary suspension of duty on Ethene
tetrafluoro-oxidized, polymerized, reduced, methyl esters
Document 3: extend the temporary suspension of duty on disperse yellow 64
Document 4: extend temporarily the suspension of duty on Disperse Yellow 64
Document 5: suspend temporarily the duty on mixtures containing Thiencarbazone-methyl
Isoxaflutole, and Cyprosulfamide
Document 6: extend the temporary suspension of duty on formulations containing Bromacil and Diuron
application adjuvants
Document 7: suspend temporarily the duty on dimethyl carbonate
polymer with 1,6-hexanediol and 2-oxepanone

topic.name: U.S. Intelligence and Defense Funding
topic.description: This topic encompasses the authorization and appropriation of funds for U.S. intelligence and defense activities across various government agencies.

Document 1: Authorizes appropriations for FY2009 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
Specifies that the amounts authorized and the authorized personnel ceilings as of September 30, 2009, for such activities are those specified in the classified Schedule of Authorizations.
Document 3: Amends provisions of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2010 relating to the Navy's littoral combat ship program
increase from 10 to 20 the number of such ships authorized to be procured.
Document 4: Amends the National Security Act of 1947 to require the Director of National Intelligence to report annually to the congressional intelligence committees
on violations of law or executive order by personnel of an element of the intelligence community.

topic.name: Telecommunications Regulation and Safety
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing the safety, accessibility, and fairness of telecommunications services in the United States.

Document 1: prohibit businesses from manufacturing or importing for use in the United States, or selling or leasing in the United States, a multi-line telephone system unless it is pre-configured to allow users to directly initiate a call to 9-1-1
Businesses are prohibited from installing, managing, or operating multi-line telephone systems without such a direct 9-1-1 call configuration.
Document 2: Directs the Federal Communications Commission to establish a program under which a wireless carrier receiving a license...may partition or disaggregate the license in order to make unused spectrum available to small carriers
Provides carriers receiving such licenses and participating in the program with a three-year extension of licenses granted under this Act.
Document 3: Directs the Federal Communications Commission (FCC)...to revise its regulations to provide that digital audio radio satellite service (DARSS): licensees shall not provide services that are locally differentiated
Requires the FCC to complete a rulemaking proceeding to determine whether DARSS licensees should be permitted to provide locally oriented services.
Document 4: Prohibits states or local governments from imposing any new discriminatory tax on mobile services, mobile service providers, or mobile service property
Defines 'new discriminatory tax' as a tax imposed on mobile services, providers, or property that is not generally imposed on other types of services or property.
Document 5: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to direct the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to adopt a final rule establishing support for broadband under the Universal Service Fund Lifeline Assistance Program
Establishes a program model under which qualifying individuals may elect to apply support from the Lifeline program to basic telephone service, voice telephony service, or broadband service.
Document 6: Directs the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to authorize owners and operators of wireless microphones...to have access to the geolocation database
Specifies registration sites for the purpose of identifying locations to be protected through database registration.
Document 7: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to make it unlawful for any person in the United States...to cause any caller identification service to transmit misleading or inaccurate caller identification information
Prohibits construing these provisions to prevent blocking caller identification or to authorize or prohibit law enforcement or U.S. intelligence agency activities.

topic.name: U.S. Congressional Elections and Transparency
topic.description: Legislation related to voting rights, election processes, and transparency in campaign financing within the United States Congress.

Document 1: Restores the right of District of Columbia residents to participate as Maryland residents in congressional elections and presidential elections.
Document 4: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise the requirement of a 48-hour notification of a campaign contribution of $1,000 or more to the Federal Election Commission.
Requires Senate candidates to file designations, statements, and reports directly with the FEC.

topic.name: Education Funding and Reform
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation aimed at improving funding, instructional quality, and educational standards in K-12 education.

Document 1: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to require local educational agencies (LEAs) to use only for direct or indirect instructional services any funds received under the program established to ensure that students make adequate yearly progress (AYP) toward state academic performance standards.
Limits to 10% the portion of such funds that LEAs may use for indirect instructional services.
Document 2: Amends part A of title I (Improving the Academic Achievement of the Disadvantaged) of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to require local educational agencies to describe how they will assist each of their elementary schools that receive school improvement funds in conducting a ready school needs review at least once every three years.
Requires the review to assess a school's needs for: developmentally appropriate curricula, classroom materials, teaching practices, instructional assessments, and accommodations.
Document 3: Amends title VI (Flexibility and Accountability) of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to create a new Part D: Promoting Youth Financial Literacy.

Allows the Secretary of Education to provide grants to states to assist local educational agencies and public schools provide financial education programs to students in kindergarten through grade 12.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Education to allot matching grants to states and, through them, subgrants to local educational agencies (LEAs), childhood education program providers, or consortia of those entities to implement high-quality prekindergarten programs for children from low-income families.
Defines 'high-quality prekindergarten programs' as those that serve children who are 3 or 4 by the eligibility determination date or have attained the legal age for state-funded prekindergarten.
Document 5: Amends the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act to reimburse private nonprofit and local government service institutions under the school lunch program for up to one meal and one snack per child each day during after-school hours, weekends, and school holidays during the regular school calendar.
Currently, such institutions are reimbursed for meals and snacks served to children over the summer months.
Document 6: Amends title II (Preparing, Training, and Recruiting High Quality Teachers and Principals) of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to create a new Part E: Building School Capacity for Effective Teaching and Leadership.

Directs the Secretary of Education to allot grants to states for subgrants to local educational agencies (LEAs) to improve teaching and school leadership through a system of teacher and principal induction, professional development, and evaluation.
Document 7: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to require states to establish challenging academic content and student achievement standards in science, beginning in the 2008-2009 school year.
Measure the achievement of students in grades three through eight against such standards.

topic.name: Wildlife and Land Management Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative measures focused on the management and protection of wildlife and natural resources in the United States.

Document 1: Prohibits any wolf in Minnesota, Wisconsin, and Michigan from being treated under any status of the Endangered Species Act
Each state shall have exclusive jurisdiction over the management of wolves within its borders.
Document 2: Directs the Department of the Interior to enter into an agreement for the management of free-roaming wild horses
Requires cost-effective management of such horses while ensuring that natural resources within the Refuge are not adversely impacted.
Document 3: Authorizes the Forest Service and BLM to enter into stewardship contracting projects to achieve land management goals
States that land management goals may include habitat for wildlife and fisheries.
Document 4: No permit shall be required for the use of a pesticide registered under the Federal Insecticide, Fungicide, and Rodenticide Act
Conduct of any other pest control activity conducted in accordance with the Plant Protection Act.
Document 5: Prohibits federal agencies from authorizing a prescribed burn on federal land during extreme fire danger levels
The United States shall be liable for damages to private properties caused by the burn.
Document 6: Designates the North Palisade as the Brower Palisade in honor of David Brower.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of the Interior and Secretary of Agriculture to submit annual reports on wildland firefighter safety practices
Includes training programs for wildland fire suppression and prescribed burning.

topic.name: Financial Regulatory Reforms
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at reforming and enhancing the oversight and regulation of financial institutions and markets in the United States.

Document 1: Audit The Fed Act of 2013 - Repeals the authority of the Comptroller General (GAO) to carry out an onsite examination of an open insured bank or bank holding company only if the appropriate federal regulatory agency has consented in writing.
Directs the Comptroller General to complete, before the end of 2014, an audit of the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System and of the Federal Reserve Banks.
Document 2: Amends the Bank Holding Company Act of 1956 relating to prohibitions on proprietary trading and certain relationships with hedge and private equity funds (Volcker Rule).
Prohibits any construction of these prohibitions to require the divestiture of collateralized debt obligations that were issued before December 10, 2013.
Document 3: Amends the Consumer Financial Protection Act of 2010 to require the Director of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) to request reports through the prudential regulator from a person that is either an insured depository institution or an insured credit union with total assets of $10 billion or less.
Prohibits the Director from making such requests for an industry-wide report, or for a report pertaining to two or more such persons.
Document 4: This bill amends the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002 to make Public Company Accounting Oversight Board disciplinary proceedings public unless the Board, on its own motion or after considering the motion of a party, orders otherwise.
Document 5: Instructs the Secretary of the Treasury to submit to Congress a list of all commercial banks, investment banks, hedge funds, and insurance companies that the Secretary believes are too big to fail.
Break up entities included on such list so that their failure would no longer cause a catastrophic effect on the United States or global economy without a taxpayer bailout.
Document 6: Amends the Federal Reserve Act to repeal the authority of a federal reserve bank to buy or sell bills, notes, revenue bonds, or warrants with a maturity date of six months issued by state or local governments.
Document 7: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act (FDIA) to prohibit an insured depository institution from being an affiliate of any broker or dealer, investment adviser, investment company, or any other person or entity engaged principally in the issue, flotation, underwriting, public sale, or distribution of stocks, bonds, debentures, notes, or other securities.
Prohibits officers, directors and employees of securities firms from simultaneous service on the boards of depository institutions.

topic.name: Border Security and Homeland Security
topic.description: The documents focus on legislation related to border security, law enforcement, and the integration of tribal governments into homeland security efforts.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Defense (DOD) to deploy at least 6,000 National Guard personnel to perform operations in the Southwest Border region
Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to increase the number of Border Patrol agents
Document 3: Establishes the Office of Tribal Government Homeland Security (Office) within the Department of Homeland Security (DHS)
Requires the Secretary to treat Indian tribes as states, as appropriate, for the purpose of homeland security
Document 5: Directs the Attorney General to expand resources for the Project Gunrunner initiative
Provide ATF agents, equipment, and training to assist Mexican law enforcement officers

topic.name: Consumer Product Safety and Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at ensuring the safety and proper regulation of consumer products, pharmaceuticals, and food items to protect public health.

1: Prohibits a person from selling to consumers any covered product that is subject to a recall.

Defines a 'covered product' to include a motor vehicle or replacement equipment, food, drugs, devices, cosmetics, a biological product, a consumer product, a meat or meat food product, a poultry or poultry product, and an egg or egg product.
2: This bill amends the Controlled Substances Act to allow a pharmacist to partially fill a prescription for a schedule II controlled substance.
It is prescribed in accordance with existing laws and regulations.
3: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to revise definitions, including defining the 'process for the review of animal drug applications.'
Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to assess 50% of the standard fee for a new animal drug application.
4: This bill amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to establish a third-party quality system assessment program.
Device manufacturers with quality systems that have been certified by an accredited person are allowed to make changes to a device without submitting to the FDA the premarket notification.
5: This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to deny a tax deduction for the cost of direct-to-consumer advertising of prescription drugs.
Defines 'direct-to-consumer advertising' as any dissemination by or on behalf of a sponsor of a prescription drug product of a prescription drug advertisement.
6: Amends the Federal Meat Inspection Act, the Poultry Products Inspection Act, and the Egg Products Inspection Act to require a person to notify the Secretary of Agriculture or the Secretary of Health and Human Services about adulterated food products.
Provides the Secretary of Agriculture with authority for voluntary and mandatory nondistribution and recall.
7: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to exempt dietary supplements containing a dietary ingredient marketed before January 1, 2007 from the requirement to provide premarket evidence of safety.

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Acts on Domestic Policy
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts focused on U.S. domestic policy, including defense contracting, animal welfare, and procurement regulations.

Document 1: U.S. policy to recognize Jerusalem as the undivided capital of the State of Israel
Requires any official U.S. document which lists countries and their capital cities to identify Jerusalem as Israel's capital.
Document 2: Requires each solicitation of offers by the Department of Defense (DOD) for a covered contract to include a requirement that the offeror include with the contract bid or proposal a written statement concerning compensation
Includes as a covered contract a contract for the procurement of goods or services in excess of $1 million.
Document 3: Amends the Animal Welfare Act to define a 'high volume retail breeder'
Requires the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to promulgate requirements for the exercise of dogs.
Document 4: Prohibits a military exchange store or other nonappropriated fund instrumentality of DOD from purchasing for resale any military decorations not produced in the United States
Provides an exception when the Secretary determines that a satisfactory quality and sufficient quantity cannot be procured.
Document 5: Prohibits heads of the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) from obligating or expending funds for projects unless all of the iron and steel used is produced in the United States
Waives such prohibition in cases where the prohibition would be inconsistent with the public interest.
Document 6: Amends the Federal Property and Administrative Services Act to extend to 15 the number of years that a federal or DOD multiyear contract may be entered into
Applicable in the case of the purchase of advanced biofuel.
Document 7: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to revise the prohibition on importing convict-made goods into the United States
Grants the President authority to recommend waiver of the applications set forth in this Act.

topic.name: Small Business Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at supporting and enhancing small business development and opportunities in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Small Business Act to reauthorize programs for small businesses administered by the Small Business Administration (SBA)
Addresses issues in management, operations, technology, finance, and other disciplines required for women's small businesses.
Document 2: Amended with respect to the requirement that federal agency heads ensure that senior executive personnel responsible for acquisition assume responsibility for small business contracting goals.
Responsible for the agency's success in achieving small business prime contracting and subcontracting goals.
Document 3: Requires unobligated funds in the Small Business Lending Fund to be transferred to the Community Development Financial Institutions (CDFI) Fund.
Directs the Administrator of the CDFI Fund to carry out a Small Business Capital Investment (SBCI) Program.
Document 4: Amends the Small Business Act to include as a base closure area for the HUBZone program a military installation's municipality, county, or census tract.
Focuses on historically underutilized business zones.
Document 5: Amends the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009 to repeal the small business stabilization program.
Provides deferred loans to small businesses experiencing difficulty in repaying existing business debt.
Document 6: Amends the Small Business Act to authorize a contract to be awarded based on competition restricted to qualified HUBZone small businesses.
Requires at least two qualified HUBZone small businesses to submit offers.
Document 7: Amends the Small Business Investment Act of 1958 to increase the maximum amount of outstanding leverage available by the SBA.
Focuses on small business investment companies not under capital impairment.

topic.name: Mortgage and Foreclosure Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at regulating mortgage practices, protecting homeowners from foreclosure, and ensuring fair treatment in mortgage-related processes.

Document 1: Requires each servicer of a home mortgage to respond in writing within 75 days to a mortgagor of a residential mortgage loan who has requested in writing a short sale
Authorizes an aggrieved individual to bring a civil action for damages and equitable relief for any violation of this Act.
Document 2: Amends the Federal National Mortgage Association Charter Act and the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation Act to increase mortgage portfolio limitations
Instructs the Director of the Office of Federal Housing Enterprise Oversight to lift the limitation on growth provision set forth in the Fannie Mae Consent Order.
Document 3: Prohibits a court from entering a deficiency judgment requiring the mortgagor to pay any balance on the mortgage loan after a foreclosure sale
Applicable to any federally related mortgage loan made after enactment of this Act.
Document 4: Amends federal bankruptcy law governing a Chapter 13 debtor to exclude certain debts from computation
Permits a Chapter 13 bankruptcy plan to modify the rights of claim holders with respect to a claim for a loan originated before the effective date of this Act.
Document 5: Reauthorizes appropriations for the Native American housing assistance block grant program through FY2020
Requires the payment of not less than prevailing wages in the administration of all federal funding for projects.
Document 6: Amends federal bankruptcy law governing a Chapter 13 debtor to exclude certain debts from computation
Allows modification of the rights of claim holders in the event of a foreclosure notice.
Document 7: Sets a deadline for the Director of the Federal Housing Finance Agency to terminate the conservatorship of either Fannie Mae or Freddie Mac
Amends the Federal Housing Enterprises Financial Safety and Soundness Act to repeal certain housing goals.

topic.name: Federal Land Conveyance and Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and actions related to the conveyance, management, and use of federally owned lands across various states.

Document 1: Replaces the map included in the set of maps entitled 'Coastal Barrier Resources System'...
Document 2: This bill requires the Department of the Interior to give permittees of specified federal land...
Document 3: Instructs the Secretary of the Interior to modify a land grant patent issued to the Great Lakes Shipwreck Historical Society...
Document 4: This bill directs the Department of the Interior to convey specified land owned and managed by the Bureau of Land Management...
Document 5: Requires conveyance of approximately 44 acres of federally owned land administered by the Agricultural Research Service...
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to convey certain federal land in Pinal County, Arizona to Resolution Copper...
Document 7: This bill authorizes Escambia County, Florida, at its discretion, to convey or dispose of all of its interest in any part of the property on Santa Rosa Island...
